rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Charging The Interests Of Judgment Debtors In Limited Liability Companies
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER CHARGING THE INTERESTS OF JUDGMENT DEBTORS IN LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES is CONTINUED to May 2, 2025 per the agreement of the parties. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 5910 INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. 1000 VAN NESS LP,1000 VAN NESS OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF 5910 INVESTMENTS, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER HEARING REQUIRED. The Court is inclined to grant the motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 564(b)(1) and (b)(9) and appoint Michael G. Kasolas as Receiver for 1000 Van Ness Owners Association. The Court is further inclined to sign the proposed order submitted by The Marquee Homeowner's Association delineating the appointment and setting forth the Receiver's powers and duties. However, the Court has questions relating to the bond amount, the amount of funds expendable without the receiver's approval, and the date for the next status conference. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. ROBERT WILSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Substitute Plaintiff And To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 24, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF MISSION ROCK RESIDENTIAL CALIFORNIA, INC. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER TO SUBSTITUTE PLAINTIFF AND TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION is DENIED. There is no competent admissible evidence that defendant is still in possession. Evidence of defendant's failure to make payments to the current owner/management company is over three months old. Additionally, there is no competent admissible evidence that the notice regarding the change in owner/management, which included the instructions for payment to the current owner/management company, was served on defendant. To the extent the moving party seeks to substitute the plaintiff, this request is denied as it is not discussed in the moving papers. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
